hints.filium: 'filium' is a noun meaning 'son' in the accusative case
hints.suum: 'suum' is a possessive adjective meaning 'her own'

hints.to prepare: 'to prepare' is 'parare' — 'Peperit' comes from 'parere' (to give birth)
hints.to endure: 'to endure' is 'perpeti' — 'Peperit' comes from 'parere' (to bear/bring forth)

hints.Present: Present would be 'parit' — 'Peperit' with reduplication (pe-) marks the perfect
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'pariebat' — 'Peperit' is the 3rd singular perfect
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect would be 'pepererat' — 'Peperit' is 3rd singular perfect

hints.paro, parare, paravi, paratum: These are the parts of 'parare' (to prepare) — a 1st conjugation verb
hints.pareo, parere, parui, paritum: These are the parts of 'parere' (to obey) — a different verb
hints.pello, pellere, pepuli, pulsum: These are the parts of 'pellere' (to drive) — also has reduplication but different verb

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'filius' — 'filium' with -um ending is accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'filio' — 'filium' is the accusative direct object
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'filio' — 'filium' is the accusative direct object of 'Peperit'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'filii' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'filii' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'filii' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'primogenitus' — 'primogenitum' agrees with 'filium' in the accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'primogenito' — 'primogenitum' is accusative modifying 'filium'
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'primogeniti' — 'primogenitum' is accusative agreeing with 'filium'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'primogeniti' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'primogeniti' ends in -i
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'primogeniti' ends in -i
